criteria.then: exit code 0 and output lists --request-id, --repo-hash, --remote, --auto as recognized arguments

criteria.given: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID=testsid is set and no grant file exists at the computed path
criteria.when: python3 scripts/execute-push.py --request-id abc123 --repo-hash $(printf '%s' "$(realpath "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)")" | sha256sum | cut -c1-16) --remote origin is run from the repo root
criteria.then: exit code is 1 and stderr contains "push-analyst did not write a grant"

expected_stderr_contains: push-analyst did not write a grant

criteria.given: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is unset; repo-hash argument is the correct computed hash for the current repo (so SESSION_ID check fires before repo-hash check per required validation order: env var checked first)
criteria.when: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID= python3 scripts/execute-push.py --request-id abc --repo-hash $(printf '%s' "$(realpath "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)")" | sha256sum | cut -c1-16) --remote origin is run
criteria.then: exit code is 2 and stderr contains "CLAUDE_SESSION_ID"

check.note: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID check MUST precede repo-hash validation to make this AC deterministic

criteria.given: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID=testsid is set, a valid grant JSON exists with "verdict":"blocked" and "risks":["test risk"] and properly matching fields (branch, head_sha, remote_name, nonce, session_id, expires_at in future), and no sentinel file exists at the expected path
criteria.then: exit code is 1 and stderr contains blocked and test risk and Chain-B sentinel file does NOT exist

criteria.then: exit code is 2 and stderr contains repo-hash mismatch or similar message

criteria.given: commands/push.md at HEAD after dev commit
criteria.then: output contains both "MUST NOT be delegated to a subagent" and either "privilege escalation" or "60-second" (or "60s")

expected_stdout_contains: MUST NOT be delegated to a subagent

criteria.then: output contains the invocation form with --request-id, --repo-hash, --remote

criteria.when: grep "rm -f.*push-analyst" commands/push.md is run, AND grep for imperative orchestrator-directed phrases in Step 4 ("Read the grant at", "Validate the following", "Consume.*unlink") is run
criteria.then: "rm -f.*push-analyst" grep exits 1 (no such line remains) OR the only occurrence is inside a comment or historical reference; AND the Step 4 imperative phrases grep exits 1 (old instructions removed); AND grep for execute-push.py in Step 4 area returns a reference to the script

commands: grep -E "Read the grant at|Validate the following fields|Consume.*unlink" commands/push.md; [ $? -eq 1 ]
check.note: All three commands must succeed (exit 0). First two must find zero matches. Third must find execute-push.py reference.

criteria.given: A temporary HOME with a fake executable ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh that prints its argv and exits 0; CLAUDE_SESSION_ID=testsid set; a valid approved grant exists with correct matching fields and expires_at in the future
criteria.when: python3 scripts/execute-push.py --request-id <request_id> --repo-hash <repo_hash> --remote origin is run with HOME pointing to the temp directory
criteria.then: script exits 0; CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID env var was passed to the fake push.sh (visible in its argv/env capture); grant file is unlinked after execution; Chain-B sentinel was written with correct JSON fields (result=PASS, request_id, head, branch, remote all populated correctly)

check.note: Positive happy-path integration test per Codex ISSUE 1. Use a fake push.sh that captures env and argv to /tmp, then exits 0. Verify sentinel existed at exec time by reading the fake script's capture log.
expected_conditions: sentinel written with result=PASS and all binding fields
fake push.sh received CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID in environment
fake push.sh received 'origin' as first positional arg

criteria.given: A valid grant exists with expires_at in format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ (trailing Z, as written by push-analyst), and all other fields correct, verdict=approved
criteria.when: python3 scripts/execute-push.py --request-id <request_id> --repo-hash <repo_hash> --remote origin is run (with fake push.sh setup as in AC10)
criteria.then: Script correctly parses the Z-suffix ISO-8601 timestamp and does NOT exit with expiry error; proceeds to sentinel-write and exec

check.note: Verifies Codex ISSUE 6 fix: expires_at Z-suffix handling. Script must normalize 'Z' -> '+00:00' or use datetime.fromisoformat-compatible parsing before comparing to datetime.now(timezone.utc).
